channel.title: TechNode
channel.description: Latest news and trends about tech in China

item.title: Look AI makes the future of fashion design real-time, AI-powered, and in your hands
item.dc:creator: Jessie Wu
item.description: Amid the growing integration of technology and fashion, AI is gradually transforming the fashion design process. Look AI, a product of Shenzhen-based tech firm, leverages advanced algorithms to provide designers with intelligent creative tools. Traditional fashion design can be both complex and time-consuming, as designers spend considerable effort on sketching, selecting fabrics, and adjusting patterns, […]
item.content:encoded:
Amid the growing integration of technology and fashion, AI is gradually transforming the fashion design process. Look AI, a product of Shenzhen-based tech firm, leverages advanced algorithms to provide designers with intelligent creative tools.

Traditional fashion design can be both complex and time-consuming, as designers spend considerable effort on sketching, selecting fabrics, and adjusting patterns, with many creative ideas taking time to materialize. Look AI meets these challenges by harnessing AI technology to streamline and optimize the design workflow, providing designers with a more efficient and accessible creative experience.

Real-time AI tools enable designers to see garment details as they draw

The core feature of Look AI is its real-time design capability. Designers can sketch on an iPad using common drawing software, and when connected to a computer, the AI product instantly converts the sketches into complete garment renderings. 

As designers draw clothing contours or lines, details such as fabrics, folds, and structure appear on the screen in real time, which creates a true “what you see is what you get” experience. This approach improves design efficiency, allowing designers to focus more on creativity without spending excessive time on technical operations.  

Supporting features: a convenient, multi-tasking toolbox

In addition to real-time design, Look AI offers a variety of supporting features. For instance, designers can upload photos of models wearing garments, and the system automatically detects and isolates the clothing, generating flat patterns for further processing. For instance, designers can upload photos of models wearing garments, so that the system automatically detects and isolates the clothing and generates flat patterns for further processing.

The tool can convert clothing photos into flat templates, apply prints directly, create textures from patterns, and intelligently adjust colors. By reducing the need to switch between multiple design tools, these features allow a complete design to be finished in as little as a few minutes.

Full-process integration from sketch to runway at industry events

At the Media Day of the first XIN Summit this November, designers sketched line drawings while the AI simultaneously produced finished renderings, adjusting details in real time to ensure accurate presentation of complex designs.

At last year’s China Lingerie Culture Week, Look AI introduced an innovative “design-as-runway” model. Once designers completed their sketches on site, the AI instantly generated a virtual runway animation and projected it onto the stage screen.

AI tools help designers work faster today and innovate for tomorrow

For independent designers and small creative labels, Look AI helps lower the barrier to entry. It speeds up the production of visual drafts and reduces costs, which allows designers to create more polished concepts at an early stage. Larger brands can use the tool to accelerate workflows, develop prototypes more quickly, or experiment with concept collections.

As its data and algorithms continue to improve, Look AI may find broader use across the industry, offering an optional tool for brands seeking to digitize parts of the fashion design process.

item.title: Sony sues Tencent’s Light of Motiram for copying Horizon, Tencent agrees to suspend all marketing and public tests
item.dc:creator: TechNode Feed
item.description: Sony and Tencent have agreed to halt all marketing and public testing for Tencent’s game Light of Motiram ahead of a major court hearing in early 2026 in Sony’s copyright lawsuit over the title. Sony filed the suit in July 2024, alleging that Light of Motiram copied character designs and survival-game mechanics from its Horizon […]
item.content:encoded: Sony and Tencent have agreed to halt all marketing and public testing for Tencent’s game Light of Motiram ahead of a major court hearing in early 2026 in Sony’s copyright lawsuit over the title. Sony filed the suit in July 2024, alleging that Light of Motiram copied character designs and survival-game mechanics from its Horizon franchise. Ahead of the key hearing, Tencent agreed to suspend all promotional activities and public tests for the game and committed not to release it before the fourth quarter of 2027. Sony’s Horizon series comprises three main titles, and the first Horizon: Zero Dawn was released in 2017. [IThome, in Chinese]

item.title: ByteDance’s first Doubao-assisted AI phone sells out at $495 as second-hand prices rise by at least $210
item.dc:creator: TechNode Feed
item.description: The Nubia M153 engineering prototype, the first smartphone to feature ByteDance’s Doubao AI assistant in a preview version, has sold out on ZTE’s official online store at a price of RMB 3,499 ($495). Because the device was released in limited quantities, resale prices have climbed sharply. On second-hand marketplace Xianyu, unopened units are listed for […]
item.content:encoded: The Nubia M153 engineering prototype, the first smartphone to feature ByteDance’s Doubao AI assistant in a preview version, has sold out on ZTE’s official online store at a price of RMB 3,499 ($495). Because the device was released in limited quantities, resale prices have climbed sharply. On second-hand marketplace Xianyu, unopened units are listed for as much as RMB 4,999 ($706) — a premium of about RMB 1,500 ($210) over the official price. The model was jointly developed by ZTE Corp and ByteDance. ByteDance’s Doubao unit led the product definition and user experience of the AI assistant, while ZTE’s Nubia brand handled hardware design, development and manufacturing. The phone comes in a single 16GB+512GB configuration and is equipped with Qualcomm’s Snapdragon 8 Elite chipset and a 6.78-inch LTPO display. [TechNode reporting]

item.title: Elon Musk says WeChat is indispensable in China, plans X aligned with WeChat
item.dc:creator: TechNode Feed
item.description: Tesla CEO Elon Musk said he aims to turn social media platform X into a multi-purpose “super app,” describing it as a “WeChat++” for markets outside China. Speaking on the People by WTF podcast, Musk said Chinese users are deeply reliant on Tencent’s WeChat, which integrates messaging, social media and payments, but noted that no […]
item.content:encoded: Tesla CEO Elon Musk said he aims to turn social media platform X into a multi-purpose “super app,” describing it as a “WeChat++” for markets outside China. Speaking on the People by WTF podcast, Musk said Chinese users are deeply reliant on Tencent’s WeChat, which integrates messaging, social media and payments, but noted that no comparable product exists overseas. He said X plans to combine content distribution with financial services, including payments, in an effort to create a one-stop platform and fill what he sees as a gap in global consumer apps. [IThome, in Chinese]

item.title: Baidu reportedly begins new round of layoffs, some teams may shrink by 40%
item.dc:creator: TechNode Feed
item.description: Baidu last week reportedly initiated a new round of workforce reductions, affecting multiple business units, a process expected to continue through the end of 2025. According to sources familiar with the matter, the exact number of layoffs will vary by business unit and performance ratings, and some teams could potentially face cuts of up to […]
item.content:encoded: Baidu last week reportedly initiated a new round of workforce reductions, affecting multiple business units, a process expected to continue through the end of 2025. According to sources familiar with the matter, the exact number of layoffs will vary by business unit and performance ratings, and some teams could potentially face cuts of up to 40%. The mobile ecosystem business is expected to be the most affected area, while positions related to AI and cloud computing are being prioritized for retention, with resources further shifted toward these sectors. Baidu is currently facing dual pressures from intensifying competition in the AI sector and declining advertising revenue. Its third-quarter 2025 financial report showed total quarterly revenue of RMB 31.174 billion ($4.42 billion), down 7% year-on-year. [Icsmart, in Chinese]

item.title: Didi launches all-day fully driverless Robotaxi service in Guangzhou
item.dc:creator: TechNode Feed
item.description: Didi’s autonomous driving unit on Monday announced the start of a 24/7 fully driverless Robotaxi trial in select demonstration zones in Guangzhou, following its deployment of autonomous services at the 15th National Games of China. Users can now request a fully autonomous ride through the Didi app, with the ability to change destinations or safely […]
item.content:encoded: Didi’s autonomous driving unit on Monday announced the start of a 24/7 fully driverless Robotaxi trial in select demonstration zones in Guangzhou, following its deployment of autonomous services at the 15th National Games of China. Users can now request a fully autonomous ride through the Didi app, with the ability to change destinations or safely stop the vehicle during trips. The trial covers high-traffic areas including subway stations, schools, and shopping centers. Passengers can also contact remote customer service via the in-car screen. The company said the trial marks a new stage in its service capabilities, with plans to expand to additional cities and accelerate the commercialization of autonomous driving services. [IThome, in Chinese]

item.title: DeepSeek launches V3.2 models with integrated reasoning tool use
item.dc:creator: TechNode Feed
item.description: DeepSeek has released the V3.2 and V3.2-Speciale models across web, app, and API. The company said V3.2 adds built-in reasoning for agent tasks and is its first model to support tool calls in both reasoning and non-reasoning modes. It reportedly reaches GPT-5-level results on public reasoning benchmarks while reducing output length and computational cost. V3.2-Speciale […]
item.content:encoded:
DeepSeek has released the V3.2 and V3.2-Speciale models across web, app, and API. The company said V3.2 adds built-in reasoning for agent tasks and is its first model to support tool calls in both reasoning and non-reasoning modes. It reportedly reaches GPT-5-level results on public reasoning benchmarks while reducing output length and computational cost.

V3.2-Speciale is available through a temporary API for research and extends long-form reasoning and mathematical proof. The model achieved gold-level results in IMO, CMO, ICPC World Finals, and IOI 2025. DeepSeek said the Speciale version consumes more tokens and does not support tool use, while the regular V3.2 is optimized for daily tasks. [TechNode reporting]

item.title: Sony to release China edition of Astro Bot on Dec. 12
item.dc:creator: TechNode Feed
item.description: Sony Interactive Entertainment said that the China edition of Astro Bot will go on sale on Dec. 12, marking the game’s official launch in the mainland market. Astro Bot is a 3D platformer in which players guide a small robot through diverse levels using various abilities to complete an adventure. Developed by Team ASOBI, the title […]
item.content:encoded: Sony Interactive Entertainment said that the China edition of Astro Bot will go on sale on Dec. 12, marking the game’s official launch in the mainland market. Astro Bot is a 3D platformer in which players guide a small robot through diverse levels using various abilities to complete an adventure. Developed by Team ASOBI, the title is a sequel to Astro’s Playroom and the third installment in the Astro Bot series. Since its global release in September 2024, the game has gained broad popularity for its varied gameplay and creative design, winning Game of the Year at The Game Awards 2024. Sony said the China edition will be available in both digital and physical standard disc versions, with a suggested retail price of RMB 298 ($42). [TechNode reporting]

item.title: China halts sales of older-standard E-bikes as new safety rules take effect
item.dc:creator: TechNode Feed
item.description: China on Monday began a nationwide halt on sales of electric bicycles that do not meet its latest mandatory safety standards, ending the availability of so-called “old standard” models. Under the updated national standard, Electric Bicycle Safety Technical Specification, all e-bikes sold from Dec. 1 must comply with the new requirements. Authorities have revoked or […]
item.content:encoded: China on Monday began a nationwide halt on sales of electric bicycles that do not meet its latest mandatory safety standards, ending the availability of so-called “old standard” models. Under the updated national standard, Electric Bicycle Safety Technical Specification, all e-bikes sold from Dec. 1 must comply with the new requirements. Authorities have revoked or cancelled all previous certifications tied to older-standard models, and retailers are barred from selling them. Violations will be subject to enforcement actions, regulators said. The change does not affect consumers who have already purchased and registered older-standard e-bikes, which may continue to be used. Manufacturers have pledged to provide at least five years of maintenance and spare parts supply for existing vehicles. [IThome, in Chinese]

item.title: China’s food-delivery price war sees Meituan, Alibaba, JD.com incur $14B in costs across two quarters
item.dc:creator: TechNode Feed
item.description: Heavy subsidy spending in China’s food-delivery battle led Meituan to report a 19.8 billion yuan ($2.7 billion) operating loss in the third quarter of 2025, its largest since listing. Alibaba’s operating profit fell from 35.2 billion yuan to 5.4 billion yuan ($4.9 billion to $0.75 billion), while JD.com recorded a 10.5 billion yuan ($1.4 billion) […]
item.content:encoded:
Heavy subsidy spending in China’s food-delivery battle led Meituan to report a 19.8 billion yuan ($2.7 billion) operating loss in the third quarter of 2025, its largest since listing. Alibaba’s operating profit fell from 35.2 billion yuan to 5.4 billion yuan ($4.9 billion to $0.75 billion), while JD.com recorded a 10.5 billion yuan ($1.4 billion) operating loss in the same quarter after reducing spending.

The three companies incurred more than 100 billion yuan ($14 billion) in costs across the second and third quarters of this year due to delivery subsidies and related sales expenses. Meituan’s sales expenses exceeded those of Pinduoduo despite lower transaction volume. Alibaba said Taobao Instant Commerce reached a 40 percent GMV share in restaurant delivery under a two-player market definition. [LatePost, in Chinese]
